--- title: "[pairwise_rmst_test()]{.doc-object-name .doc-function .doc-label .doc-label-function}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} Pairwise RMST tests for all group pairs with multiple-comparison correction. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/_rmst.py#L586-L716){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.FUNCTION} ```python pairwise_rmst_test( surv, tau, group, *, estimand="difference", strata=None, correction="holm", conf_level=0.95, format=None ) ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} Compares RMST between all pairs of groups, with optional multiple-comparison adjustment. This answers the question: "Which pairs of groups have significantly different RMST?" when you have more than two groups. ::: ## Parameters {.doc-parameters} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [surv]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Surv]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A right-censored `Surv` response (time-to-event data). [tau]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : The restriction time for RMST calculation. [group]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Group labels, one per observation. Can be array-like or categorical variable. Must have at least 2 unique levels to create pairs. [estimand]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [["difference"]{.st}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Type of estimand: `"difference"` (default), `"ratio"`, or `"percentage_difference"`. [strata]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any | None]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : (Optional) Stratification variable. Each pairwise test is stratified by this factor. [correction]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [["holm"]{.st}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Multiple-comparison adjustment: `"holm"` (default), `"bh"`, `"bonferroni"`, or `"none"`. [conf_level]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[0.95]{.fl}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Confidence level for intervals (the default is `0.95`). [format]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str | None]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : Output format: None (auto-detect), `"pandas"`, `"polars"`, or `"pyarrow"`. ::: ## Returns {.doc-returns} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [DataFrame]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : One row per pair of groups with columns `group1`, `group2`, `rmst1`, `rmst2`, `estimate`, `se`, `lower_ci`, `upper_ci`, `statistic`, `p_value`, and `p_adjusted`. ::: ## Details {.doc-details} All $\binom{k}{2}$ pairwise comparisons are performed, where $k$ is the number of unique group levels. The raw p-values are then adjusted for multiplicity using the chosen correction method. Holm's step-down procedure (the default) controls the family-wise error rate without assuming independence between tests. ## Examples {.doc-examples} Compare one-year RMST across sex groups in the lung cancer dataset with Holm correction: ::: {#30161a9d .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw # Load data and build a right-censored response lung = gw.load_dataset("lung", backend="polars") y = gw.Surv.right(lung["time"], event=(lung["status"] == 2)) # Run pairwise RMST comparisons with Holm-adjusted p-values gw.pairwise_rmst_test(y, tau=365, group=lung["sex"], format="polars") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html}
